title: FT.SYNUPDATE
description: Creates or updates a synonym group with additional terms

params.description: Terms to add to the synonym group
params.name: terms

redis_py.signature: synupdate(groupid: Union[str, int], skipinitial: bool = False, *terms: str)

jedis.signature: String ftSynUpdate(String indexName, String synonymGroupId, String... terms)

go-redis.signature: FTSynUpdate(ctx context.Context, index string, synGroupId interface{}, terms ...interface{})

node_redis.signature: SYNUPDATE(index: RedisArgument, synonymGroupId: RedisArgument, terms: Array<RedisArgument> | RedisArgument, options?: { SKIPINITIALSCAN?: boolean })

params.description: Whether to skip the initial scan
params.name: skipInitialScan

returns.description: true if the synonym group was updated

nredisstack_sync.signature: bool SynUpdate(string indexName, string synonymGroupId, bool skipInitialScan = false, params string[] terms)

params.description: Whether to skip the initial scan
params.name: skipInitialScan

returns.description: true if the synonym group was updated

nredisstack_async.signature: Task<bool> SynUpdateAsync(string indexName, string synonymGroupId, bool skipInitialScan = false, params string[] terms)

content:
Update a synonym group [Examples](#examples) ## Required arguments
index is index name.
synonym_group_id is synonym group to return.
Use FT.SYNUPDATE to create or update a synonym group with additional terms. The command triggers a scan of all documents. ## Optional parameters
SKIPINITIALSCAN does not scan and index, and only documents that are indexed after the update are affected.
## Examples
Update a synonym group {{< highlight bash >}} 127.0.0.1:6379> FT.SYNUPDATE idx synonym hello hi shalom OK {{< / highlight >}} {{< highlight bash >}} 127.0.0.1:6379> FT.SYNUPDATE idx synonym SKIPINITIALSCAN hello hi shalom OK {{< / highlight >}}
## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis Cloud
Flexible & Annual | Redis Cloud
Free & Fixed | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="ft-synupdate-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} One of the following: * [Simple string reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-strings" >}}): `OK` if executed correctly. * [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: no such index. -tab-sep- One of the following: * [Simple string reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-strings" >}}): `OK` if executed correctly. * [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: no such index. {{< /multitabs >}} ## See also [`FT.SYNDUMP`]({{< relref "commands/ft.syndump/" >}}) ## Related topics [RediSearch]({{< relref "/develop/ai/search-and-query/" >}})
example:
127.0.0.1:6379> FT.SYNUPDATE idx synonym hello hi shalom
OK

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="ft-synupdate-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

One of the following:
* [Simple string reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-strings" >}}): `OK` if executed correctly.
* [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: no such index.
